model._hint: Transformer: city96/FLUX.1-schnell-gguf (flux1-schnell-Q8_0.gguf, ~12 GB). Pipeline components (VAE, text encoders, scheduler): black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell. Q8_0 keeps near-bfloat16 quality with trivial dequantisation overhead on MPS. sequential_cpu_offload is disabled (incompatible with GGUF). | gguf_file points to the transformer weights only. base_model_id supplies all other components from the gated FLUX.1-schnell repo (HF token in .hf_token required).

generation._hint: 1–4 steps. 4 gives the best quality/speed balance. 1 step sufficient for rough drafts. | CRITICAL: guidance_scale MUST be 0.0 — FLUX.1-schnell is latent-adversarial-distilled (similar to SDXL-Turbo). Any positive CFG value degrades quality severely. | Up to ~1 MP. 1024×1024 is the standard. 512×512–1024×1024 all work well. Arbitrary resolutions are supported.

system._hint: ~12 GB peak; sequential_cpu_offload: disabled (incompatible with GGUF)

notes.about: Generates high-quality general-purpose images in just 1–4 steps using the FLUX.1-schnell model. Ideal for rapid iteration across a wide range of subjects — portraits, landscapes, objects, and abstract concepts.
notes.prompt_guide: No trigger word. Write detailed descriptive prompts — negative prompts are ignored at guidance_scale=0.0. Examples: 'a golden sunset over mountain peaks, cinematic lighting, wide angle', 'portrait of a young woman, soft natural light, photorealistic', 'a futuristic city street at night, neon reflections, rain'.
